title: v0.1 Alpha Release
description: Shipped across the v0.1.x releases. Gate for making the repo public and sharing with coworkers. Language surface is honest — named arguments (#56), agents nest in modules (#63), types usable from agents (#70), enum variant construction completeness (#96), capability checks cover test blocks (#104) — schedule handlers fire on their own (#71), agent events survive crashes (#72), every spec example compiles or is marked Planned (#77), the first-five-minutes DX holds up (assertion failures say why #105, skein new git-inits #106, zsh completion #101, MCP compile_check tells the truth #109), and releases/badges/versioned docs are automated for public consumption (#100).

title: v0.2 Beta Release
description: Shipped as v0.2.0. Post-alpha hardening, informed by alpha feedback: replay backend injection (#73), the capability-parameter surface decision (#69) and parameter-scoped enforcement for process/timer/event.log (#57), process.spawn task bodies (#74), enum value-level exhaustiveness warnings (#76), local LLM backends for dev via OpenAI-compatible servers + skein.toml profiles (#107), LSP code actions from fix_hint/fix_code (#108), and the Int string-interpolation fix (#114).

title: v1.0.0-rc Release
description: The gate for tagging v1.0.0-rc.1 — output of the 2026-06-11 release-readiness pass. Shipped: every item fixed and v1.0.0-rc.1 tagged 2026-06-12. NOTE (2026-06-15 / 2026-06-19 roadmap resets): rc.1 was tagged prematurely. The 2026-06-14/15 dogfooding audit (skein-testing + FablePool ports) and the 2026-06-19 source-verified soundness/contract audit reset the GA picture — v1.0.0 is no longer an imminent RC promotion. The next release is the development milestone v0.4.0, not another RC. See docs/ROADMAP.md.

title: v0.4.0 — Truth & Soundness
description: First reset wave (1.0 blockers). Wave A — Truth reset & surface cuts (remaining): de-reserve or justify `resume` (#301); add the missing E0028/E0029 rows to spec §7 + docs-site errors.md (#312); `!`/`?` position standardization + get!/put! removal (#268); flakes #271 (fix on main — close after green-CI observation) and #272. Wave B — Analyzer/codegen soundness: COMPLETE (B1–B6 / #290–#295 merged by 2026-07-01; source-verified and adversarially probed by the 2026-07-02 sanity check — `docs/audits/2026-07-post-wave-b-sanity-check.md`). The analyzer-accept ⇒ BEAM-load bridge held against every probe; provider contract checking (E0038) and transitive purity landed in B6. Wave B residue (the 'silently cross' half of the invariant), filed 2026-07-02 as v0.4.0 blockers: #309 bare `Ok`/`Err` as a value compiles silently to an atom; #310 interpolation segment types unchecked (accepted programs crash `{:unsupported_interpolation, v}`); #311 newline-`(` juxtaposition parse + misleading diagnosis (grammar decision before freeze); #313 E0020 fallback fix_code is a `//` placeholder. Exit: Wave A items + residue closed, negative fixtures pinned for each, full suite green (baseline 2026-07-02: 2,363 tests + 209 properties, 0 failures).

title: v0.5.0 — Runtime Contract & Dogfood
description: Second reset wave (1.0 blockers). Wave C — Runtime ABI, schema, capability authority, storage honesty: make the runtime expose exactly the contract the analyzer and spec claim. Open blockers from the 2026-06-19 audit: C1 authoritative effect-ABI matrix / single source of truth (today three hand-maintained copies drift — `timer.cancel` is Result[String,String] in the analyzer, bare `:ok` at runtime, `-> ()` in spec); C2 one structured-error ABI (LLM/tool/provider errors leak raw Elixir structs behind opaque enums — `LlmError.ProviderError(..)`/`ToolError.ValidationError(..)` CANNOT match; store-put/memory-put are bare strings; blocked-live raises are intentionally uncatchable — document/freeze that); C3 one recursive schema engine (`llm.json[T]` does NOT validate, only parse+atomize; tool output never validated; uniqueItems/formats/nesting are dead); C4 scenario-provider soundness (runtime authority DONE; analyzer provider-contract checking + transitive purity landed in B6/#295 — #279 is narrowed to llm.embed provider support + the `given`-grammar reconciliation); C5 honest store contract (untyped dynamic store, dead Ecto typed-table path, id not Uuid-checked — direction pre-committed 2026-07-02: TYPED tables with generic declared PK types — #255); C6 EventStore persistence + frozen shapes (SQLite is an orphaned parallel backend; ordinary append is ETS-only; durability claim is false; restart not genuinely tested). Wave D — Dogfood conformance & release gates (#262): CI compiles, loads, and RUNS reduced, pinned programs from Skein examples + skein-testing + FablePool-skein, asserts real Result/error/schema/store shapes, detects API drift (ambient `Uuid.new()` was REMOVED from the stdlib, so both external ports are known-broken against main today with no gate saying so — verified 2026-07-02), compiles every spec/docs `skein` fence (#202 subset), snapshots complete structured diagnostics, and widens the B4 soundness-property generator (#314). Factor-quality companion sequenced with C1: #315 (registry extraction out of analyzer.ex, pass-group submodules, AST.Assert). Observability CLI work (#284 render/JSON/trace-crash fix) already landed. Minimal canonical substrate is NOT in this milestone — #300 resolved 2026-07-02 as Alternative B (promise dropped): the substrate items stay in v1.1 and there is no v0.6.0 milestone.

title: v1.0.0-rc.2 — True release candidate
description: Cut a real RC only once every 1.0 blocker in v0.4.0 + v0.5.0 is green and the conformance gate executes the reduced testbeds end-to-end (#300 resolved as B: no canonical-substrate milestone). Gate input: the agent-writability benchmark (#320) reports in release-readiness. No feature work in this milestone — only freeze verification, blocker fixes, docs corrections, migration fixes, and release mechanics. This replaces the premature rc.1 framing: no new RC until the new 1.0 gate is close to green. Run the exact release-readiness workflow used for GA; soak the same candidate before promoting to v1.0.0.

title: v1.0.0 Release
description: The GA tag — NOT imminent (reset 2026-06-15, re-baselined 2026-06-19). Freeze only after all preceding contracts are executable and green. GA is a sound, honest, deterministic, dogfood-proven core: (1) sound analyzer/codegen/runtime (no analyzer-success crash/miscompute; no `:unknown` at public boundaries; true `?`; tagged records/Option); (2) typed/honest capabilities & effects with one effect-ABI matrix and one structured-error ABI; (3) scenario-scoped capability environments (analyzer + runtime); (4) deterministic/replayable scenario/golden testing; (5) one recursive schema engine across request/LLM/tool-in/tool-out; (6) honest store + EventStore contracts; (7) minimal canonical bytes/hash substrate ONLY if 'FablePool-capable' stays an explicit 1.0 promise (open decision); (8) dogfood conformance across Skein, skein-testing, and FablePool-skein as a continuous gate. Tags once v1.0.0-rc.2 has soaked and every blocker is closed. Application/memory policy (provenance, lineage, grants, redaction) stays OUT of the language.

title: v1.1: Hardening & Language
description: Post-1.0 language + effect work intentionally cut from the minimal 1.0 surface (default home for deferred items). Closures/anonymous functions (#248), record update (#251), module constants (#249), enum variant payloads (#247), `via`/`via Module` if still useful after scenario environments, behavioural stateful stubs, general effect-provider interfaces, content-addressed store convenience APIs (#255 convenience half), effectful crypto — secure RNG/keygen/signing (#257), `llm.rerank` (#145), docs/spec drift guards (#202), and the LSP structural-navigation work (#240). Home for the canonical-bytes/hash/encoding substrate (#245/#256/#246/#250) — #300 resolved 2026-07-02 as Alternative B, so these stay here. A specific item promotes to 1.0 only when a checked-in executable conformance program proves it indispensable.

title: v1.2: Interop & Agent Workflows
description: Bigger design efforts after the hardening wave: Erlang/Elixir FFI via extern (#141, explicitly cut from 1.0 — the 1.0 crypto route, if any, is a narrow internal `:crypto` wrapper, never general FFI), human-in-the-loop approval workflows with suspend before sensitive tool calls (#144), the browser-based trace viewer (#143), the Raxol TUI for an interactive CLI (#171, separately gated from the framework-neutral CLI work), and native structural search/codemod (#241).

title: Future: Platform
description: Deliberately deferred platform bets, parked until the core language and runtime stabilize: hot code upgrades (#142), the managed deployment platform (#148), and the tools/connectors marketplace (#149). Re-scope each from scratch when pulled into active work.
